memoryArtifacts.headline: Ambrose Fowler and Jane Alvord History follows:
memoryArtifacts.description:
AMBROSE FOWLER was born about 1618, of Dalbury, Lees, Derbyshire, England, to William Fowler (1572-1660) and Sarah Neeld (1599-1638.) He married Jane Alvord, 26 May 1646, in Windsor, Massachusetts.

A …

memoryArtifacts.headline: William Fowler 1590-1661
memoryArtifacts.description:
William FOWLER [Parents] was born about 1590 in Dalbury Lees, , , England. He died on 25 Jan 1660/1661 in Milford, , Conn.. William married Sarah.

    REFERENCE: Typed genealogy of Mrs. C. E. DeWick, …

zh.Dalbury Lees, Derbyshire, England: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾達爾波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Tapp於1599年出生在英國德比夏爾達爾波利時，她的父親Edmund Tapp Jr.21歲，母親Mary Anne Charde16歲。她於1620年與Judge William Fowler I of Milford在英國德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1638年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德享年39歲。

zh-hans.Dalbury Lees, Derbyshire, England: 英国英格兰德比夏爾達爾波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Tapp于1599年出生在英国德比夏爾達爾波利时，她的父亲Edmund Tapp Jr.21岁，母亲Mary Anne Charde16岁。她于1620年与Judge William Fowler I of Milford在英国德比夏爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1638年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Tapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa). The unrecorded Old English personal name is thought to be the first element of the placenames Taplow (Buckinghamshire), Tapners (Kent), Tappington (Kent), and Tapton (Derbyshire). See also Taplin .
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English tape ‘ribbon, tape’, or tappe ‘plug, tap’; ‘tap, knock’.

variants.name: Tappe
meanings: North German: metonymic occupational name for the owner of a tavern, a beer or wine tapper, from Middle Low German tappe ‘tap’.
English: variant of   Tapp  . This surname has now died out in Britain.

variants.name: Taplin
meanings: probably from Middle English Tolpayn, which may be a nickname for a miller, from Middle English tollen ‘to exact a charge on (something)’ + Middle English (Old French) pain ‘bread’, though the tolls that the miller exacted were on the flour he ground not the bread that was made from it. Alternatively, if -payne is a spelling of Middle English (Old French) peine ‘tax’, it may instead be a nickname for a tax collector.

variants.name: Tapscott
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from a lost Devon placename, perhaps in or near Barnstaple, recorded as Tappyscote in 1504–15. The placename may derive from an early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa), genitive Tappes, + Middle English cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’, but this cannot be certain due to a lack of medieval forms. See also Tapp for the Middle English personal name Tapp, which is most frequent in Devon.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Tapp was born in 1599, in Dalbury, Derbyshire, England, her father, Edmund Tapp Jr., was 21 and her mother, Mary Anne Charde, was 16. She married Judge William Fowler I of Milford in 1620, in Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1638, in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 39.

fr.Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony,: Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Tapp est née en 1599, à Dalbury, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Tapp Jr., avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mary Anne Charde, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Judge William Fowler I of Milford en 1620, à Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1638, à Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Tapp 1599 in Dalbury, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Tapp Jr. 21 und ihre Mutter Mary Anne Charde 16. Sie hat 1620 in Derbyshire, England Judge William Fowler I of Milford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1638 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Tapp(이)가 1599년 영국 더비실르 다버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Tapp Jr.(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Anne Charde(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1620년 영국 더비실르에서 Judge William Fowler I of Milford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1638년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Judge William Fowler I of Milford em 1620, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony,: милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Tapp родилась в 1599 г. в местности далбури, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Tapp Jr. было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Anne Charde, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Judge William Fowler I of Milford в 1620 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1638 г. в местности милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 39 лет.
